key_insight: The paper extends mean field control to deterministic policies with joint state-control distributions, enabling a model-free continuous-time actor-critic method with measure-aware policy gradients.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical reinforcement learning framework for systems where collective behavior and control interactions matter, such as crowds, swarms, and finance with trade crowding.
method: Derive a model-free sensitivity formula for McKean–Vlasov dynamics.
Express the policy gradient via advantage-rate and local value/advantage-rate functions on Wasserstein space.
Build a continuous-time deep deterministic policy gradient algorithm using particle approximations, measure-dependent neural networks, and temporal-difference learning.
evidence_signal: The method is validated in numerical experiments on stochastic Cucker–Smale consensus control and optimal liquidation with trade crowding, showing efficiency, stability, and robustness.
watch_item: Implementation may be challenging because the algorithm requires accurate particle approximations and learning measure-derivative terms for joint state-action distributions.
paper_title: Actor-Critic Learning for Extended Mean Field Control with Deterministic Policies
